{{Quote|Cargo ferry ''Sela'' is carrying the TIE Defender prototypes and will rendezvous with escort carrier ''Hininbirg'' near Vinzen Neela 5.|An [[Imperial briefing officer]]|audio=6M2I1.flac|<ref name="TIE Fighter">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref>}}
'''Vinzen Neela 5''' was an astronomical object<ref name="TIE Fighter" /> located within the [[Vinzen Neela system/Legends|Vinzen Neela system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']]<ref name="Atlas" /> [[Parmic sector/Legends|Parmic sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="TIE Fighter Date">{{TIEFighterDate}}</ref> following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the [[cargo ferry]] group [[Sela (cargo ferry group)|''Sela'']] was set to rendezvous with the [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|''Ton-Falk''-class escort carrier]] ''[[Hininbirg]]'' near Vinzen Neela 5 to transfer the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defender]] prototypes that the former was carrying. [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] forces arrived in the area before the Imperial [[Starship/Legends|starships]] could in an attempt to [[Battle of Vinzen Neela 5|ambush]] them and disrupt the transfer. In response, Imperial [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were dispatched to protect the transfer operation.<ref name="TIE Fighter" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
In the ensuing battle, both ''Sela'' and the ''Hininbirg'' arrived in the area and the prototypes made their way to the latter, while all Rebel ships and [[Mine Type A|mines]] were destroyed by Imperial starfighters. By the end of the battle, all Imperial ships had exited the area via [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], with the ''Hininbirg'' taking the prototypes to<ref name="TIE Fighter" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="TIE Fighter" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
Vinzen Neela 5 first appeared in [[Totally Games]]' [[1994]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]. The astronomical object was shown on a map in the briefing for the sixth Tour of Duty's second mission, titled "Prevent Rebel Ambush."<ref name="TIE Fighter" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Vinzen Neela [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Vinzen Neela 5, in grid square P-18.<ref name="Atlas" />
